topic: Theme 2: Political and Artistic Culture in the German-speaking World
subject: german

items.why: Students translate word by word; it means freedom of the press, a core political-culture value.

items.q: The Berlinale belongs to which area of culture?
items.why: Students don't link events to art forms; the Berlinale is a flagship film festival.

items.q: Which is part of political culture in the German-speaking world?
items.correct: Public debate and a free press
items.why: Students confuse grammar with cultural content; press freedom and debate are genuine political-culture themes.

items.q: Goethe is a central figure of German:
items.why: Correct answer: Literature. “Painting” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
